description: NVDA AI assistant helps NVDA users by using AI to simplify complex information, summarize content, and provide contextual guidance for tasks that are otherwise hard to access.
changelog: added fixes for chat ui and improved error handling when gemini API key is invalid or missing

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
Package: MarkupSafe (whl)
Version: 3.0.3
Description: Safely add untrusted strings to HTML/XML markup.

The provided source code is a feature-rich NVDA (NonVisual Desktop Access) add-on named 'AI-assistant'. Its primary purpose is to provide accessibility enhancements for visually impaired users by integrating Large Language Models (LLMs) into the screen reader environment. The plugin allows users to generate summaries of web pages, obtain descriptions of the current foreground window (via screenshots), and interact with an AI through a dedicated chat interface. It supports both local model execution via Ollama and cloud-based processing via Google's Gemini API. The package bundles several legitimate open-source libraries as dependencies, including Jinja2, Pygments, Python-Markdown, and MarkupSafe. The core logic involves extracting text and structural metadata from the user's current focus, processing it through the selected LLM, and presenting the output in a browseable format. The code uses standard NVDA APIs for UI interaction and script handling, and standard Python libraries for networking and image processing. No malicious behavior, such as unauthorized data exfiltration, hidden remote code execution, or suspicious background activity, was detected. All operations, including network requests and screen captures, are directly tied to the application's stated functionality and are triggered by user actions.
